reference: Your body has systems designed to close the type of hole that occurs during an ablation. An ablation is done by touching part of the heart with a catheter. The catheter produces local changes that kill the tissue. Parts of the heart are very thin, sometimes only a few millimeters. Therefore, a puncture is one of the complications that sometimes occurs and is not always avoidable. Once closed, it will heal. The pre-existing hole is unrelated to the one created by the catheter and was not likely changed, but only your doctor who performed the procedure can answer accurately.
